name: tooltip
title: Tooltip
description: Minimal tooltip with hover delay and terminal styling. Built on the Popover primitive with full 4-side positioning and collision avoidance. Shows contextual information on hover without clipping by overflow ancestors.

docs.description: Minimal tooltip with hover delay and terminal styling. Built on the Popover primitive with full 4-side positioning and collision avoidance. Shows contextual information on hover without clipping by overflow ancestors.

anatomy.name: Tooltip
anatomy.note: Root — manages hover state, delay, and enabled toggle

anatomy.name: Tooltip.Trigger
anatomy.note: Element that activates the tooltip on hover/focus

notes.title: Shorthand
notes.content: Use the `content` prop on Tooltip root for simple text tooltips without compound nesting: `<Tooltip content="Help text"><button>…</button></Tooltip>`.

notes.title: Positioning
notes.content: Content supports all four sides via the `side` prop: `top` (default), `bottom`, `left`, `right`. Use `align` (`start`, `center`, `end`) for cross-axis alignment. Automatic flip and shift when content would overflow the viewport.

notes.title: Portal Rendering
notes.content: Content renders via Portal at document.body level, so tooltips are never clipped by overflow:hidden ancestors. Position is calculated from trigger's bounding rect.

notes.title: Conditional Display
notes.content: Use the `enabled` prop to conditionally disable the tooltip without unmounting. Pairs well with useOverflow — only show tooltip when content is actually truncated.

notes.title: Delay
notes.content: Default show delay is 500ms (hover intent). Hide is instant. Customize via `delayMs` prop.

notes.title: Built on Popover
notes.content: Tooltip is a thin wrapper around the Popover primitive with `triggerMode="hover"`. For interactive floating content, use Popover directly.

notes.title: Controlled Mode
notes.content: Supports `open`, `defaultOpen`, and `onOpenChange` props for programmatic control: `<Tooltip content="Help" open={showHelp} onOpenChange={setShowHelp}>...</Tooltip>`.

keyboard.description: Tooltip shows on focus and hides on blur, so Tab into the trigger reveals the tooltip. Escape dismisses the tooltip and returns focus to the trigger.
